name: Excessive File Access Events From the Same Username
notes:
This rule triggers when at least 15 different files are accessed by the same username within 5 minutes. 

Note: Edit the AQL function to exclude known legitimate download activities such as OS Updates or Software Updates.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Object Access Events
when the event matches Username is not N/A, Filename (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches NOT ((NETWORKNAME(destinationaddress)='Net_172_16_0_0') OR (INCIDR('192.0.2.0/24', destinationaddress))) AQL filter query
when at least 15 events are seen with the same Username, Log Source and different Filename (custom) in 1 minutes
thresholds: when at least 15 events are seen with the same Username, Log Source and different Filename (custom) in 1 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Excessive File Access Events From the Same Username on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Object Access Events
AND when the event matches Username is not N/A, Filename (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches NOT ((NETWORKNAME(destinationaddress)='Net_172_16_0_0') OR (INCIDR('192.0.2.0/24', destinationaddress))) AQL filter query
AND when at least 15 events are seen with the same Username, Log Source and different Filename (custom) in 1 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Excessive File Access Events From the Same Username
Event description: Detected excessive file accesses from the same username
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
